name: Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop
name_original: Cueva de los Casares y valle de los Milagros — circular por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo
description: Hike a moderate 8.0-mile loop in Alto Tajo Natural Park, exploring Cueva de los Casares, Valle de los Milagros, and diverse geology.
region_intro: Hiking around Riba De Saelices offers diverse terrain within the Sierra del Ducado, situated at the boundary between the high plains of Alta Alcarria and the Sistema Ibérico. The region features an average altitude exceeding 1000 meters, characterized by varied geological formations including sandstone and limestone. The Linares River carves through the landscape, creating valleys and riparian areas. This environment provides a mix of canyons, gorges, and unique rock formations for exploration. Best hiking trails around Riba De Saelices The most popular hiking route is Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the notable Valle de Los Milagros and its geological features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Buenafuente del Sistal Monastery Loop, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the local landscape and historical sites. Local hikers also love the Tagüenza Bridge loop from El Altillo, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Riba De Saelices is defined by the Valle de Los Milagros, the Linares River, and a dynamic landscape of canyons, gorges, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, with all 6 available routes rated as moderate. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 5 hikers have used komoot to explore Riba De Saelices's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Riba de Saelices?
A: The hiking trails in Riba de Saelices offer a diverse landscape, characterized by the Sierra del Ducado. You'll encounter varied geological formations, including both sandstone and limestone, which create dynamic features like canyons, gorges, and unique rock formations. The Linares River also carves through the area, providing picturesque riparian landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes in Riba de Saelices?
A: All 6 available hiking routes around Riba de Saelices are rated as moderate. While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this guide, these moderate trails offer a rewarding experience for those with a reasonable level of fitness.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for hikes in this region?
A: The hikes around Riba de Saelices generally range from approximately 5.8 miles (9.3 km) to 9.1 miles (14.7 km). You can expect these routes to take between 2 hours 43 minutes and 4 hours 22 minutes to complete, depending on the specific trail and your pace.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Riba de Saelices?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of Miracles&lt;/a&gt;, carved by the Linares River, which showcases diverse geological elements. Other notable features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4320256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña River Gorges&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6039679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recuevano Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The varied terrain also features canyons, gorges, and unique rock formations.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses into the area's history. For instance, the surroundings of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares&lt;/a&gt;, a globally renowned cave with Paleolithic engravings, also feature a Hispano-Muslim settlement and an Islamic tower. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagüenza Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4272783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Riba de Saelices suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: While all routes are rated moderate, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030461685/cueva-de-los-casares-and-valley-of-miracles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that explores the notable Valle de Los Milagros. The recreational area at the foot of the Cueva de Los Casares offers amenities like parking and picnic tables, making it a convenient starting point for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Riba de Saelices?
A: The natural environment of Riba de Saelices, with its diverse landscapes and open spaces, is generally welcoming for dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations before heading out.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riba de Saelices?
A: Riba de Saelices offers a great outdoor experience throughout much of the year. The diverse terrain and natural features are enjoyable in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are preferable. The area's varied landscape also provides unique beauty during winter, though some routes might be more challenging.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the routes in this guide are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030461685/cueva-de-los-casares-and-valley-of-miracles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares and Valley of Miracles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030761699/buenafuente-del-sistal-monastery-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Buenafuente del Sistal Monastery Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tagüenza Bridge loop from El Altillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park when visiting the hiking trails?
A: A convenient parking area is available at the recreational area located at the foot of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de Los Casares&lt;/a&gt;. This spot serves as a starting point for the "Valle Los Milagros" route and offers amenities like picnic tables.

Q: What makes Riba de Saelices a unique hiking destination?
A: Riba de Saelices is unique due to its location at the boundary of the high plains of Alta Alcarria and the Sistema Ibérico, resulting in an average altitude exceeding 1000 meters. It's part of the Geoparque Molina-Alto Tajo, emphasizing its significant geological features, including diverse rock types, canyons, and gorges. The area's rich biodiversity and historical sites like the Cueva de Los Casares further enhance the hiking experience.

Q: How many hikers have explored Riba de Saelices using komoot?
A: More than 5 hikers have already used komoot to explore the varied terrain and beautiful landscapes of Riba de Saelices, discovering its unique natural and historical attractions.
tips: One of the most important caves in the center of the peninsula for its large number of prehistoric engravings.
Valley eroded and sculpted by the passage of the Linares River for many years. This valley runs for several kilometers through areas of sandstone and slate, several of them of great beauty.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this circular route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. It requires good physical condition due to its length and varied terrain, including uphill sections and frequent river crossings. Carrying walking poles is highly recommended for assistance.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike?
faqs.answer: The route can be started either from Riba de Saelices or from a picnic area located near the Cueva de los Casares. Both offer convenient access points for the trailhead.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a Natural Park, specific regulations for dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It's always best to check with the Alto Tajo Natural Park authorities directly regarding leash rules and any restrictions before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this route is generally during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and river crossings might be more challenging after heavy rains.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is typically no general entrance fee for the Alto Tajo Natural Park itself. However, visiting the Cueva de los Casares, with its significant Paleolithic rock art, requires advance booking. It's advisable to check the park's official website for any specific regulations or permits that might apply to certain activities or areas.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural and archaeological wonders. Key highlights include the Cueva de los Casares, famous for its Paleolithic rock art, and the stunning Valley of Miracles, known for its unique rock formations like Puntal del Milagro and Peña Eslabrada. You'll also experience diverse geological landscapes, from red sandstones to dark slate, and frequent crossings of the Linares River.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and captivating. You'll traverse a mix of narrow ravines and wider valley sections, with geological shifts from reddish sandstones to dark slate. The path involves frequent crossings of the Linares River, sometimes over small stone bridges or stepping stones, and can include uphill sections and less defined paths amidst riverside trees.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route also passes through sections of 'El valle de los Milagros' and 'Carril de los Milagros', offering a comprehensive experience of the area's natural beauty.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and river crossings, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, plenty of water, snacks, and especially walking poles for stability during river navigation. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route starts near Riba de Saelices, which is a small village. While specific amenities like cafes or restaurants directly at the trailhead are not detailed, you may find basic services in Riba de Saelices. It's always a good idea to pack your own food and drinks.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, length (over 12 km), varied terrain, and frequent river crossings, this trail might be challenging for younger children or families not accustomed to longer, more strenuous hikes. An out-and-back section between the Cueva de los Casares and Cueva de la Hoz is noted as being 'fresh and comfortable' for part of its length, which might be a more suitable option for families looking for a shorter, less demanding experience.
